pr.title: sobjlib: decompile SObj setup and render paths
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Decompiled major SObj setup/render paths in sysdolphin baselib: HSD_SObjLib_803A477C and HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC reached 100%, while HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 was added as a 99.88707% near-match. The work added typed SObj descriptor/color fields, texture/TLUT setup logic, GX/TEV render-state programming, and small Dolphin GX SDK type adjustments needed by the implementation. Verification included pre-commit, configure+ninja, object rebuild, and objdiff evidence for the changed functions and data.
what_changed: Added a full C implementation of HSD_SObjLib_803A477C, allocating an HSD_SObj, initializing primary and optional secondary texture objects, handling optional TLUT-backed CI textures, setting default transform/color/state fields, computing normalized texture extents, linking the SObj to a GObj, and returning the initialized object.

Added a large C implementation of HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 for SObj rendering. The function configures GX vertex descriptors, texture loading, Z texture behavior, culling, TEV stages, alpha/blend state, per-format color/alpha behavior, rotated quad geometry, texture coordinates, and final HSD/GX state cleanup.
Converted HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC from a near-match/todo state to a 100% match, including use of a volatile load from HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98 for the 2.0f far value.
Added data constants in sobjlib.c for TEV/k-color values and float constants 0.5f and 2.0f, including HSD_SObjLib_804DEA80 through HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98.
Defined SOBJLIB_INTERNAL before including sobjlib.h, implying the header exposes internal struct details only to the implementation.
Expanded sobjlib.h with SObj descriptor/color typing required by the new implementations.

Updated Dolphin GX texture format typing by folding GX_TF_C4, GX_TF_C8, and GX_TF_C14X2 into GXTexFmt instead of a separate GXCITexFmt, and changed GXInitTexObjCI declarations/definitions to take GXTexFmt.
Added extern/.clang-format with DisableFormat: true and SortIncludes: false to prevent auto-formatting or include sorting in the external SDK area.
Made a small tobj.c adjustment related to the GX texture format type changes.
smart_moves: Used SOBJLIB_INTERNAL to keep internal SObj layout/type exposure scoped to sobjlib.c rather than broadly exposing private implementation details.

Matched GXInitTexObjCI to the real usage pattern by accepting GXTexFmt, allowing CI texture formats and ordinary texture format values to share one enum where the decompiled code expects a single format field.
Preserved external SDK formatting with a local extern/.clang-format disabling formatting and include sorting, reducing noisy or authenticity-breaking changes under extern/dolphin.
Used objdiff percentages in the PR body to clearly distinguish fully matched functions from the remaining near-match: 803A477C 100%, 803A4A68 99.88707%, 803A55DC 100%, and .data/.sdata2 100%.
Used a volatile const f32 load from HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98 for the 2.0f constant in HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC, a targeted matching tactic for codegen/data reference behavior.
Centralized render constants as named address-derived symbols rather than raw literals throughout the new TEV/color setup.
decomp_lessons: For baselib render paths, matching often requires modeling both high-level object behavior and exact GX/TEV state sequencing; cleanup calls such as HSD_StateInvalidate, HSD_StateInitTev, HSD_ClearVtxDesc, and restoring Z mode are part of the function’s observable codegen/state pattern.

Texture format typing in SDK headers can block decompilation if the inferred game structs store one shared format type; adjust headers cautiously when multiple Dolphin APIs actually consume the same enum values.

Optional secondary textures and TLUT-backed image paths should be represented in the data model rather than forced through placeholder fields; this PR added descriptor/color typing to support cleaner setup/render code.
When a float literal causes mismatch, loading it through an address-backed const, and in this case volatile, can reproduce the original compiler behavior.
Near-matches should be explicitly retained as such when evidence shows they are not yet exact; this PR reports HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 at 99.88707% rather than overstating completion.
External SDK directories may need local clang-format controls to avoid formatter churn that obscures decomp-relevant type/header changes.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing address-style symbol names for unknown baselib functions and data, such as HSD_SObjLib_803A477C, HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68, HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC, and HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98.
Used field names that encode struct offsets where semantics are not fully known, such as x50_texobj, x70_tlutobj, x7C_texobj, x38_color, x3C_color, and x4C_callback.

Used descriptive local variable names for inferred render/setup roles, including image, tlut, image2, inv_width, inv_height, origin_x, origin_y, tex_s, tex_t, tex_s_max, tex_t_max, obj_width, obj_height, and tex_fmt.
Used HSD_SObjDesc, HSD_ImageDesc, and HSD_Tlut names for typed SObj setup inputs, consistent with SysDolphin baselib naming.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Reached 100% on HSD_SObjLib_803A477C by expressing allocation, assert, TLUT/texture initialization, default field initialization, flag setup, reciprocal texture-size math, and list insertion in C.
Reached 100% on HSD_SObjLib_803A55DC by replacing a direct 2.0f literal with `*(volatile const f32*) &HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98`, forcing the desired constant/data access behavior.
Used explicit casts such as `(u8) image->mipmap` and float casts around width/height calculations to match expected argument and arithmetic types.
Used `PAD_STACK(0x20)` in HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68, indicating a deliberate stack-shape/codegen matching aid for the large render function.
Used packed writes/casts for GXColorS10 and GXColor setup, such as assigning through `((u32*) &tev_color)` and `*(u32*) &k_color`, to reproduce the original data movement for TEV color constants.
Represented 0.5f and 2.0f as address-backed constants HSD_SObjLib_804DEA94 and HSD_SObjLib_804DEA98, contributing to .data/.sdata2 matching.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text evidence is the PR summary/verification note; no reviewer intent or requested changes can be inferred.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete the HSD_SObjLib_803A4A68 near-match from 99.88707% to 100%.
Search for SOBJLIB_INTERNAL usage to see how internal baselib struct exposure is handled elsewhere.
Search for volatile const f32 matching tactics around sdata2 constants in other sysdolphin modules.
Check follow-up changes to GXTexFmt/GXCITexFmt to confirm whether merging CI formats into GXTexFmt remained stable.
Look for later renames of SObj offset fields such as x40 flags, x38_color, x3C_color, x50_texobj, x70_tlutobj, and x7C_texobj.
